SEARCH
Module storm.core
Package st.orm.core.repository.impl

Class DirtySupport<E extends Entity<ID>,ID>

java.lang.Object
st.orm.core.repository.impl.DirtySupport<E,ID>
Type Parameters:
E - the entity type
ID - the entity identifier type
public final class DirtySupport<E extends Entity<ID>,ID> extends Object
Determines which fields of an entity are considered dirty for update purposes.

This class compares the current entity instance with the state that was observed when the entity was read within the same transaction.

The result of this comparison is interpreted according to UpdateMode:

  • UpdateMode.ENTITY: column-level dirty checking is used only to decide whether any change exists; if so, a full-row UPDATE is generated.
  • UpdateMode.FIELD: column-level dirty checking is used to select which columns are included in the UPDATE.

The default update mode can be configured via the storm.update.default_mode property (see StormConfig).

When dynamic updates are enabled, different UPDATE statement shapes may be generated depending on which fields are dirty. The storm.update.max_shapes property limits the number of distinct shapes that may be generated. Once the limit is exceeded, a full-entity update is used to preserve batching efficiency.

Dirty checking is performed for all updatable columns of the entity. This includes any record components that map to columns that can be updated (as defined by Column.updatable()). Non-updatable columns are ignored.

Dirty checks are performed via the entity Metamodel for each updatable column. If a generated metamodel implementation is available (Java annotation processor / Kotlin KSP), the generated, type-specific equality checks are used to avoid reflection and prevent boxing for primitive fields. If no generated implementation is available, reflective accessors are used and type-appropriate equality checks for primitive fields are applied where possible.

By default, dirty checking is based on identity. A field is considered dirty as soon as its extracted value is no longer identical. Value-based dirty checking can be enabled by setting the storm.update.dirty_check property to value (see StormConfig). In this mode, values are compared by semantic equality after a fast identity check when applicable.

The dirty checking strategy can also be configured per entity using DynamicUpdate.dirtyCheck().

When performing dynamic updates, the version field is always included if present.

Since:
1.7

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    DirtyCheck
    getDirtyCheck(RecordType recordType)
    Returns the effective DirtyCheck strategy for the given record type.
    int
    getMaxShapes()
    Returns the maximum number of distinct update shapes that may be generated when dynamic updates are enabled.

  • Method Details

    • getUpdateMode

      public static UpdateMode getUpdateMode(@Nonnull RecordType recordType, @Nonnull StormConfig config)
      Returns the effective UpdateMode for the given record type using the given config for defaults.

      If the record type is annotated with DynamicUpdate, the annotation value is used. Otherwise, the configured default update mode applies.

      Parameters:
      recordType - the entity record type.
      config - the Storm configuration to use for the default update mode.
      Returns:
      the effective update mode for the given record type.

    • getUpdateMode

      public UpdateMode getUpdateMode(@Nonnull RecordType recordType)
      Returns the effective UpdateMode for the given record type.

      If the record type is annotated with DynamicUpdate, the annotation value is used. Otherwise, the globally configured default update mode applies.

      The default update mode can be configured via the storm.update.default_mode property (see StormConfig). If the property is not set, the default is UpdateMode.ENTITY.

      Parameters:
      recordType - the entity record type.
      Returns:
      the effective update mode for the given record type.

    • getDirtyCheck

      public DirtyCheck getDirtyCheck(@Nonnull RecordType recordType)
      Returns the effective DirtyCheck strategy for the given record type.

      If the record type is annotated with DynamicUpdate and the DynamicUpdate.dirtyCheck() attribute is set to a value other than DirtyCheck.DEFAULT, that value is used.

      Otherwise, the globally configured dirty check strategy applies.

      The default dirty check strategy can be configured via the storm.update.dirty_check property (see StormConfig). If the property is not set, the default strategy is DirtyCheck.INSTANCE.

      Parameters:
      recordType - the entity record type.
      Returns:
      the effective dirty check strategy for the given record type.

    • getMaxShapes

      public int getMaxShapes()
      Returns the maximum number of distinct update shapes that may be generated when dynamic updates are enabled.

      This value is configured via the storm.update.max_shapes property (see StormConfig). It limits the number of different column combinations that UPDATE statements may be generated for.

      If the limit is exceeded, a full-entity update is used to avoid excessive statement fan-out and preserve batching efficiency.

      Returns:
      the maximum number of allowed update shapes
